response.justification: Candidate has relevant geospatial domain exposure (GISCollective, Green Map) and prior high-throughput, low-latency systems work at Sociomantic, plus solid engineering fundamentals (TDD, CI/CD). However, the role's primary requirement — modern C++ (or Rust) in commercial settings — is barely represented: only ~6 months of C/C++ at National Instruments over a decade ago, with no Rust experience and no navigation/routing pipeline background. Recent work is predominantly TypeScript/Node.js. Worth a screening call only if the team is open to a strong DLang systems engineer who would need to ramp on C++.
strengths: Geospatial/GIS domain experience via GISCollective and Green Map
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work at Sociomantic (millions of req/sec)
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, distributed systems exposure
concerns: Minimal modern C++ experience; no demonstrated Rust experience
No navigation/routing systems or map-tile pipeline background
Recent 5+ years focused on TypeScript/Node.js web stacks, not systems engineering
